mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise concurrently, with a hit rate below 50% over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, resulting in negative forward returns despite rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth across all rolling 1-year windows since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.35% month-over-month, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force equity valuations lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.35% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative hit rate for 20-day forward returns when both series show positive growth, invalidating the demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of rising short-term interest rates signals a manufacturing contraction exacerbated by tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where industrial production falls while the federal funds rate rises.
